== Core Structure

content.content:
== Antipatterns

[[mcm_antipattern]]
{mcm_antipattern}::
A named, recurring document defect formally catalogued so that
{mcm_curation_action_s}
and reviews can cite it precisely.
Naming the defect is what makes it detectable and dischargeable;
an unnamed defect recurs because no rule can forbid it.

[[mcm_tidemark]]
{mcm_tidemark}::
An
{mcm_antipattern}:
version commentary embedded in a durable document — revision markers, dated change narration, "new in vX" annotations.
The name is the gestalt: the stain showing where the water once stood, saying nothing about where it is.
History-time knowledge belongs to the commit;
git log and blame carry every line's date and reason with a forcing function, while in-document
{mcm_tidemark_s}
drift and pollute present-tense reading.
A durable document — spec, guide, code, context file — speaks of itself only in the present tense.
A dated artifact — memo, scar, commit message — is itself a historical record and may carry revision metadata without fault.
Detection and drain moves are catalogued in ACG (ACGm_107).

== Document Elements

[[mcm_fundamental_text]]
{mcm_fundamental_text}:: 
The basic sentences and phrases that comprise document text.
{mcm_fundamental_text}
follows specific formatting patterns in source while maintaining natural flow in rendered output.

[[mcm_notional_snippet]]
{mcm_notional_snippet}:: 
An exemplar that guides
{mcm_curation_action_s}.
While it may contain asciidoc noncompliance, the
{mcm_digital_mind}
uses it to understand patterns and generate compliant asciidoc output.

[[mcm_line_break]]
{mcm_line_break}::
A deliberate separation in a
{mcm_document}
that creates clear boundaries while preserving fluid rendering.
The
{mcm_line_break}
appears between independent sentences, before and after
{mcm_linked_term_s},
and between fragments surrounding
{mcm_linked_term_s}.
This pattern enables clean ancestry tracking while maintaining natural document flow.

[[mcm_blank_line]]
{mcm_blank_line}::
A purposeful demarcation in the
{mcm_document}
that separates major structural elements such as definition entries, sections, and lists.
The
{mcm_blank_line}
appears only between these elements, never within them.

== Term Management Elements

input.new_string:
== Word Selection

Prefix discipline governs the left half of every persistent name;
this section governs the right half — the word itself.
The rules below bind the word part of every
{mcm_quoin},
the display text of every
{mcm_linked_term},
and any persistent name a system mints: verbs, nouns, file words, command words.
They constrain births, not the living —
existing names are swept only by deliberate remint tooling, never piecemeal.

* *Semantic uniqueness.*
One meaning per word across all persistent names, repo-wide.
A word already bound to a meaning anywhere in the system is unavailable for a second meaning, however distant the domain.
* *Vocabulary isolation.*
Words mint from source domains alien to software, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and corporate project management.
A word with heavy ambient presence in software prose is a
{mcm_trodden_word}
and is disqualified regardless of semantic fit.
Domain choice makes collision structurally impossible;
word-level checks merely verify.
* *The grep gate.*
A candidate word is verified by repo-wide grep before adoption;
a clean landing is a minting precondition, not a nicety.
* *Rare but real.*
Prefer rare, real words whose true meaning does semantic work.
Concrete, physical, sensory images lodge across sessions in ways abstract labels cannot.
Abbreviations spend recognition to save characters — the wrong trade.
* *Mint into an* {mcm_asterism}.
A new word joins a coherent metaphor family wherever one fits;
a lone orphan word is weaker than a family member.
* *Audible register.*
Sibling subsystems ride
{mcm_asterism_s}
whose registers are distinguishable by ear,
so a stray term's home system is audible without lookup.

[[mcm_asterism]]
{mcm_asterism}::
A named metaphor family whose source-domain relationships mirror the system relationships it names.
The name is the gestalt: a recognizable figure of stars that is not itself a constellation and may span several —
as a vocabulary family may span
{mcm_concept_model_s}.
Each term inherits relational meaning from the family
(the coronet sits below the firemark as the hoof marking sits below the brand),
so the metaphor performs structural work beyond retrieval,
and a new term in an established family arrives nearly pre-learned —
for the
{mcm_digital_mind}
and the
{mcm_editor}
alike.
Founding specimen: Job Jockey's equestrian vocabulary (JJS0, Equestrian Vocabulary), with the racing and dressage layers as sibling families sharing one register.
Model-side rationale: `Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`;
prefix-side sibling: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`.

== Antipatterns

[[mcm_antipattern]]

+a persistent name minted from vocabulary worn featureless by ambient software prose —
+open, close, run, lock, task, manager, handler, grant.
+The name is the gestalt: a path so heavily walked that no single footprint is findable on it;
+grep returns a thousand strangers.
+A
+{mcm_trodden_word}
+fails retrieval for the
+{mcm_digital_mind}
+and search for the
+{mcm_editor},
+and invites the plan-versus-product bleed that vocabulary isolation exists to prevent.
+Semantic fit never rescues it:
+the word is disqualified before fit is weighed.
== Document Elements
[[mcm_fundamental_text]]

input.new_string:
See minting memo Pattern J (RBS0/JJS0 empirical study) for evidence.

### Word Selection Discipline (MCM "Word Selection")

Prefix rules govern the left half of a name; these govern the word itself. When minting any persistent name — quoin word-parts, verbs, nouns, colophon words:

- **Semantic uniqueness** repo-wide: one meaning per word, ever.
- **Vocabulary isolation**: no *trodden words* (`mcm_trodden_word`) — vocabulary heavy in ambient software prose (open, close, run, lock, task, grant) is disqualified regardless of fit.
- **Grep gate**: repo-wide grep must land clean before adoption.
- **Rare but real**: distinctive words whose true meaning does semantic work; concrete over abstract; never abbreviations.
- **Mint into an asterism** (`mcm_asterism`): join a coherent metaphor family with an audible register (equestrian, ecclesiastical, diplomatic, civic).

Full doctrine: MCM "Word Selection". Constrains births, not the living.

### Minting Workflow

- Domain-specific suffixes may evolve per project

### Quoin Sub-Letter Discipline (MCM Spec-Internal)

For MCM concept model specs, apply this discipline on top of the general minting rules:

- **Uniform shape `prefixXY_word` or `prefixXYZ_word`** — every quoin carries two or three sub-letters; pick one count per spec
- **Hard 3-letter ceiling** — never mint 4+ letter sub-prefixes
- **Within-domain Y monosemy** — each sub-letter has exactly one meaning per domain; reusing Y for a second concept produces pattern-recognition collisions (JJS0's `rd` appearing in three quoins with three meanings is the canonical failure)
- **Documented legend** — sub-letter table declared in a comment block at the top of the spec's mapping section; future minters consult the table rather than re-derive from first letters
- **Family members sharing YY is intended** — uniqueness lives in the full quoin name, not the 5-char prefix

See minting memo Pattern J (RBS0/JJS0 empirical study) for evidence.

### Minting Workflow

Before minting new prefixes:
1. **Enumerate namespaces** — list every place this name will appear (code, refs, commands, env vars, target paths...)
2. **Check reserved suffixes** — ensure the suffix matches intended type
3. **Verify terminal exclusivity** — search existing trees, check the memo
4. **Document the allocation** — add to prefix map in relevant heat/spec

### Project Prefix Registry

input.params: {"identity": "Bd", "files": ["Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc", "CLAUDE.md"], "intent": "MCM gains the Word Selection doctrine — the word-part complement to prefix discipline, formalizing scattered practice (JJS0 vocabulary isolation, the Quire memo's Close rejection and grep-clean verification, the citizen memo's semantic-uniqueness rule, the introspection memo's word-part craft) into six binding rules: semantic uniqueness repo-wide, vocabulary isolation from software-adjacent source domains, the grep gate, rare-but-real words, minting into an asterism, audible register separation. Two quoins mint: mcm_asterism (a named metaphor family whose source-domain relationships mirror system relationships — the figure-of-stars gestalt chosen because constellation already means the concept model itself; founding specimen JJS0's equestrian vocabulary) and mcm_trodden_word (antipattern: a name from vocabulary worn featureless by ambient software prose — grep returns a thousand strangers; disqualified before semantic fit is weighed). CLAUDE.md gains the operational echo beside the sub-letter discipline plus a word-selection step in the minting workflow. Doctrine constrains births, not the living."}

lastPrompt: Go ahead and improve MCM now and notch!  Then advise whether we ought to whittle down the job jockey section since MCM carries more weight now.

content.text:
Landed — commit `91853ce29` under ₣Bd. MCM now carries the **Word Selection** doctrine (six rules, placed just before Antipatterns), two new quoins minted — `mcm_asterism` (the metaphor family; "constellation" was already MCM's word for the concept model itself, so the figure-of-stars neighbor that *spans* constellations was the honest mint) and `mcm_trodden_word` (the antipattern, joining tidemark in the catalog) — plus the CLAUDE.md echo and a new word-selection step in the minting workflow.

**On whittling JJS0 — yes, but only one of its two layers.** The Equestrian Vocabulary section is really two things:

1. **Generalized doctrine** — the "Vocabulary Isolation" subsection's universal claims: planning systems face the plan/product bleed, alien source domains make collision structurally impossible, sensory metaphors lodge across sessions. This is now restated in MCM with more weight, and two authoritative wordings of one doctrine is exactly ACG's paraphrase-fork word-cancer. This layer should collapse to a pointer at MCM Word Selection / `mcm_asterism`.
2. **JJS0's own content** — which must stay: the JJK origin story ("in early incarnations… planning concepts became load-bearing in two systems simultaneously" — that's the *empirical evidence*, JJK's history, not doctrine), the specific claim that equestrianism shares zero terminology with software, and all the term-by-term glosses (racing and dressage layers). That material is the founding specimen MCM now cites — gutting it would leave MCM pointing at a stub.

So the whittle is modest: maybe a third of the Vocabulary Isolation subsection collapses into "this is the founding specimen of MCM's `mcm_asterism` discipline; see MCM Word Selection for the general doctrine," and everything term-specific survives. It's small, judgment-flavored, low urgency — worth a slated pace (₣Bd fits, as doctrine-consolidation follow-through) rather than doing it hot now.

And the thread we left open: the doctrine you just ratified formally indicts three of ₣BZ's five cinched civic verbs — **grant**, **revoke**, **rekey** are trodden words (grant/revoke being IAM's own vocabulary makes them maximally so), while *enfranchise* and *expel* pass every gate. The natural next move is re-minting those three inside the civic register. Want to take that up?

message.content:
Yes, do the JJS whittle deftly this turn.

Excellent!  yes, propose alternatives for those very words.  Actually, I wonder if they ought to be 'citizen/ census/ civic' constellation gestalt?  Federation seems to be tipping that way.

content.content:
* *docket* — the presentation term for the `text` field. What the {jjdpr_pace} should accomplish. Used in CLI output labels and slash command descriptions.

These are display/CLI terms, not schema changes. Field renames are deferred to a future heat.

== Upper API

Job Jockey has two distinct API layers.
The *lower layer* is the {jjdx_tool} set: boring, heterogeneous verb names implemented in Rust ({jjdo_enroll}, {jjdo_orient}, {jjdo_close}, etc.).
The *upper layer* is the equestrian vocabulary that humans use to direct a language model ({jjsuv_slate}, {jjsuv_mount}, {jjsuv_wrap}, etc.).

The separation is deliberate and follows the vocabulary isolation principle described in Equestrian Vocabulary.
Shared vocabulary between layers caused the LLM to conflate them — guessing tool parameters by analogy, reaching for raw tools instead of following workflows.
Boring lower-layer names resist pattern matching.
Vivid upper-layer names stay memorable for humans and unambiguous for language models.

The {jjsuc_insert} is the primary artifact that translates between layers — most verbs are delivered via the CLAUDE.md verb table.
V4 reserves slash commands for protocol-heavy orchestration (school, corral).
The human speaks equestrian vocabulary; the LLM invokes {jjdx_tool_s} via MCP.

=== Verbs

Upper API verbs map equestrian vocabulary to {jjdx_tool_s}.
Some verbs map directly to a single {jjdx_tool}; others orchestrate multiple tools.
All verbs are delivered via the {jjsuc_insert} verb table.

[[jjsuv_slate]]
//axl_voices axi_cc_claudemd_verb
{jjsuv_slate}::
Add a {jjdpr_pace} to a {jjdhr_heat}.
Maps to {jjdo_enroll}.

[[jjsuv_reslate]]
//axl_voices axi_cc_claudemd_verb
{jjsuv_reslate}::
Revise a {jjdpr_pace_p} docket.
Maps to {jjsoprd_revise_docket}.

[[jjsuv_wrap]]
//axl_voices axi_cc_claudemd_verb
{jjsuv_wrap}::
Mark a {jjdpr_pace} complete.
Maps to {jjdo_close}.

[[jjsuv_rail]]
//axl_voices axi_cc_claudemd_verb
{jjsuv_rail}::
Reorder {jjdpr_pace_s} within a {jjdhr_heat}.
Maps to {jjdo_reorder}.

[[jjsuv_furlough]]
//axl_voices axi_cc_claudemd_verb

input.old_string:
Job Jockey's terminology is drawn entirely from equestrianism.
This is not decoration — it is a namespace discipline applied to human cognition and language model disambiguation.

Planning systems face a persistent hazard: when the vocabulary used to _organize_ work overlaps with the vocabulary used to _do_ work, the boundary between plan and product dissolves.
"Task" could be a planning unit or a runtime construct.
"Phase" could be a workflow stage or a build step.
"Diff" could be a review artifact or a git object.
Every ambiguous term forces a context switch — the human pauses to resolve which layer a word belongs to, and the language model may silently resolve it wrong.

In early incarnations, Job Jockey used generic planning terms that bled directly into source code.
Planning concepts became load-bearing in two systems simultaneously: changing one risked breaking the other.
The fix was not better naming within the same domain — it was choosing a source domain so alien to software engineering that collision becomes structurally impossible.

Equestrianism shares zero terminology with software, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, or corporate project management.
No codebase has a `heat` object.
No ticketing system uses `firemark`.
No CI step is called `corral`.
When a human says "mount," there is no ambiguity about which system they are addressing.
When a language model encounters "school," it does not reach for a compiler.

The vocabulary also exploits a property of human memory: concrete, physical, sensory metaphors lodge more durably than abstract labels.
A jockey wearing silks, a horse being schooled in a dressage ring, a volte circling back to the same point with refined intent — these images persist across sessions in ways that "human-claimed-pending-interactive-work" cannot.
The cognitive cost of re-establishing context drops when the terms are vivid and the metaphor is coherent.

JJS0 states only what is Job Jockey's own.

Planning vocabulary is maximally exposed to the trodden-word hazard:

The racing and dressage layers below are sibling asterisms sharing the equestrian register.

content.text:
Both candidates survive the gate clean — and the sweep surfaced one genuine constraint. Here's the proposal.

**On the asterism question: civic yes, but it's more precise than that — the family that's been self-assembling is *English manorial*, and "census" is the one word to avoid.** Look at what's already minted on the GCP side: **Manor**, **levy**, **Terrier** — and a terrier, in its real meaning, *is* the manorial land-register, the book recording who holds what of the lord's land. That's not a coincidence you chose; it's the asterism doing structural work already. Citizen, enfranchise, expel, polity, seat extend it; federate/treaty language tips the federation canon the same way. But **census** is semantically bound in your own corpus to the *retired* concept — "We never build a census" is a cinched paddock line, and the Quire memo records "Census → Terrier." Minting it for anything now would collide with that meaning. So: the register is civic-manorial, anchored by Manor/Terrier, census left in its grave.

**The three replacements, minted into that family:**

| Today (trodden) | Proposal | The gestalt |
|---|---|---|
| grant | **enfeoff** | Feudal law's act of putting a tenant in possession of a holding in exchange for service — bestowing a named bundle on a citizen of the polity. The terrier is literally where feoffments were recorded: the ledger-write-then-bind ordering falls straight out of the metaphor. |
| revoke | **escheat** | The *lawful reversion* of a holding to the lord when tenure ends. Not punitive, not wrongful — which matches the verb's calm semantics (report-only surplus, idempotent, safe). The capability returns to the polity rather than being destroyed. |
| rekey | **recut** | Keys and seals are both physically *cut*. Concrete, sensory, grep-clean, and honest about the object — the key itself is GCP's literal artifact at the Palisade, not ours to rename. |

Enfeoff/escheat is a genuine legal pair — creation and reversion of tenure — so the relationship between the words mirrors the grant/revoke symmetry exactly. Runners-up, one line each: *rescind* (clean grep, but registerless modern-legal); *attaint* (clean, but punitive — could someday serve an expel-for-cause); *vest* (finance-trodden); *charter* and *warrant* fail the gate (RBYC_CHARTER exists; "warrant" drowns in license-header WARRANTIES).

Three residuals, named honestly:

- **WRC stem adjacency.** The cnmp Ward Realm lenses already bind *Fief, Steward, Vassal* in their own feudal register. `enfeoff` the verb doesn't collide with `Fief` the WRC noun, but the stem is shared across projects — and note your paddock's federation-era "manor-scope **steward**" idea has the same adjacency. If that bothers you, the fallback for grant is weaker (rescind-class words).
- **Escheat's direction.** Historically escheat *befalls* on death or felony rather than being wielded at will, though transitive use ("the crown escheated the lands") is attested. The reversion semantics are right; the agency is slightly bent.
- **Rekey is the least-trodden of the three** (GCP itself says "rotate," not "rekey") — it's defensible to keep if you want one literal word in the set. My lean is recut, for full register coherence.

One adjacent placeholder while we're here: the paddock's `read-Terrier` is unminted — *consult* the terrier would be period-correct and grep-clean, but that can wait.

If enfeoff/escheat/recut sit right, the next move is updating the ₣BZ paddock's verb table and cinch list — say the word and I'll make the edit.

lastPrompt: Yes, I'm ready to commit to these words.  However, I'm aware there may be some other words to en-quoin: there is a new bucket in the manor, and specific files writeable by governors, readable globally…

content.text:
Both reserve candidates are clean (`muniment`, `cartulary`). Here's the answer your RBS0 precedent gives, and it's crisper than I expected:

**RBS0 already runs a deliberate two-register practice.** Side by side in the same `rbtgi_` family: **Manor**, **Depot**, hallmark, reliquary — asterism words — and right beside them **RB Build Bucket**, **RBRA File**, **JSON Key File** — flat compounds. So the precedent isn't "everything interesting" or "everything precise"; it's a decision rule, and it matches the load-bearing-complexity litmus plus the introspection memo's Zipf principle:

> A word earns an *interesting* mint when the concept is ontological — it carries relationships, recurs across many specs, and the metaphor can do structural work. It stays a *precise compound* when its description exhausts its identity — plumbing referenced from few places, where a vivid word would be decoration.

Applying that to your three new artifacts:

**The per-polity ledger file — no new word needed; it already has its interesting name.** Each file simply *is* that polity's **terrier**. And I'd commit to the singular reframe while we're in the paddock: historically every manor kept *its own* terrier — no realm-wide terrier ever existed. The metaphor literally encodes your no-global-bookkeeping cinch. The paddock's current "the Terrier is a collection" framing is the one spot where the metaphor is bent; "a terrier per polity, hosted together" straightens it. The collection needs no name — "the terriers."

**The bucket — merely precise, for now: the Terrier bucket.** Its contents are homogeneous (depot terriers, and per the Quire memo, Cloister terriers join the same collection), so the compound rides the existing mint with zero learning cost, exactly like RB Build Bucket. The interesting upgrade exists and greps clean if the bucket ever outgrows terriers: **muniment** — the muniment room/chest is where a manor kept precisely these documents (terriers, charters, deeds; evidence-of-rights). I'd *name the reserve and not spend it*: minting muniment today fails the "does this element earn its existence" test, but recording it means the day a second record-kind arrives, the word is waiting.

**The ACL shapes — precise, and probably not nouns at all.** "Governor writes exactly its own terrier" is verb-spec mechanics; RBS0's precedent for recurring mechanics is the operational-pattern quoin (`rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket` — flat, descriptive). If the write-ACL pattern recurs across enfeoff/escheat/levy specs it earns one of those, in the precise register.

One flag before I touch the paddock: you said the files are "readable **globally**" — the paddock cinches the opposite: read population is governors + payor initially (admin-plane), widen only when a working verb demonstrates need, never `allUsers`/`allAuthenticatedUsers` because citizen names are reconnaissance data. Did you mean a genuine widening (all citizens of the manor?), or just "readable across the admin plane"? If it's a widening, that's a cinch change and I want it to be deliberate.

Once you answer that, I'll make the paddock edit in one pass: enfeoff/escheat/recut into the verb table and cinch list, the terrier singular reframe, the Terrier-bucket naming with muniment held in reserve.

message.content: That's a fine distinction... I hadn't thought through that.  Did we actually elect name 'Citizens' for this role?  I think I like the idea that only Directors can access, if we can use IAM access controls to hide visibility to this file.  Read the bedrock memo to anticipate name collisions while we are at it, that builds upon this.

content.text:
Three threads — the citizen election, the read-ACL question, and the bedrock collision sweep. Taking them in order:

**Yes, "citizen" is genuinely elected.** It's not just paddock prose: the heat itself is named `rbk-14-mvp-citizen-model`, the federation canon defines the tier contrast as citizen ↔ federate (D1), the citizen memo records the mint explicitly ("Citizen — a keyfile-tier operator identity… rank-neutral; the home of the identity, not the role" — including the note that it out-minted RBSHR's older prose use of the word), and the Quire memo's §12 builds choristers on it. It's load-bearing across four documents.

**On "only Directors can access" — I want to check one thing before agreeing, because the cinched design points the other way.** The paddock's read population is *governors + payor* (the admin plane), and the citizen memo's one hard security finding was that the terrier must sit **out of director reach** — the build bucket was disqualified as its home precisely because directors hold `objectCreator` there. That was about *write*; read is milder, but the stated rationale for tight reads is that citizen names are reconnaissance data, and directors are the highest-churn credential class. So: if "Directors" was a slip for "the admin plane" (governors + payor), that's the existing cinch and nothing changes. If you literally mean the director capability-set should read terriers, that's a deliberate widening — possible, but I'd want the working verb that demonstrates the need, per your own cinch.

Either way, **the IAM mechanics deliver what you want**: with uniform bucket-level access, a citizen holding zero permissions on the Terrier bucket gets full invisibility — not "access denied," but no evidence the objects exist. **Managed folders** (GA) give per-depot IAM grain: each depot's governors read/write exactly their own terrier's folder, payor reads all. The write side already implied this grain ("each depot's governors write exactly their own depot's file"), so read rides the same mechanism for free. That likely settles the paddock's open "managed-folder vs per-depot-prefix" question in favor of managed folders.

**Bedrock memo collision sweep** — I read it earlier this session; here's what the new mints touch:

- **No hard collisions.** Enfeoff, escheat, recut, Terrier bucket, muniment-in-reserve are all disjoint from Quire/Outpost/endow/dissolve/raise/strike/precentor/chorister/prebend/cloister/intone.
- **One adjacency worth electing consciously: endow vs enfeoff.** Both are medieval bestowal verbs, one per register. They're semantically distinct in exactly the right way — you *endow* an institution (a chantry, a Quire: funding it into existence) and you *enfeoff* a person (seating them in a holding) — so the register separation is doing its job, but two grant-flavored verbs is the kind of pair that deserves a deliberate "yes" rather than a drift-in.
- **A symmetry gap the memo exposes:** the chorister's ledger entry has a noun — *prebend* — but the citizen's terrier entry has none. The manorial slot-filler would be *feoffment* (the recorded act/estate enfeoffment creates). I'd leave it unnamed until the RBS0 incorporation pass, but the gap is now visible.
- **Two stale lines in the bedrock memo once the rename lands:** §12 says choristers are "enfranchised, **granted**, **revoked**, expelled," and later "Establishment (**invest/divest/roster**/endow) is control-plane REST" — the second is using cult verbs the paddock had already retired when the memo was written. Both become breadcrumbs owed at paddock-update time.
- The AWS rekey-analog line ("rides whichever identity tier") inherits *recut* cleanly.

So the one thing standing between us and the paddock edit: **who reads the terrier** — admin plane as cinched, or a deliberate directors widening?

input.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣BZ

## Boundary — shape, not mechanism (cinched)

This heat builds the **citizen tier** — the keyfile, no-org operator-credential tier —
restructured so its capability layer is identical to the future federation tier's.
The backing stays keyfile; a no-org operator runs it unchanged.
We do **not** build federation here.
OUT of scope: workforce pool/provider setup, STS exchange, IdP device flow,
RBRD mode-enum activation,
the full drift-audit machinery,
and `modifiedGrantsByRole` topology conditioning —
all post-MVP (RBSHR "Operator federation"; `Memos/memo-20260609-federation-canon.md`; audit section below).

Pre-MVP there is **no compatibility surface**: no depots exist that we must migrate.
The grandfather bootstrap (role-from-SA-name recovery) is retired unbuilt.

Mechanism lives in `Memos/memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model.md`
(superseded-in-part banner landed; consult the paddock first on Terrier homing, census, mantle, migration — and on the verb words, re-minted below)
and `Memos/memo-20260609-federation-canon.md`; this paddock holds shape.

## The premise — human present (cinched; earmarked for RBS0)

Canon D2, restated as the bounding premise:
**a human is present at the kickoff of every run; no run outlives a session cap.**
Therefore the system holds no refresh token anywhere beyond the payor's own,
ships no unattended-run support,
and an orchestrating agent never holds a secret
(device-flow kickoff surfaces only user_code + verification URI).
This belongs in RBS0 as a premise quoin voiced `axk_premise` when the civic vocabulary incorporates —
it is the constraint that bounds the entire credential design.
The unchosen middle credential path — a bash-managed per-operator OAuth refresh token —
is recorded as declined under this premise
(a durable secret at rest, plus the OAuth-client ceremony multiplied across operators);
no current revisit trigger.

## The civic structure (cinched)

**Payor founds, governors populate, terriers tell, IAM enforces.**

- **Admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into.**
  The payor's founding gestures: establish manor, levy depot, create the depot's terrier, seat the first governor.
  The payor is outside the citizenry — no terrier entry; its authority is constitutive, not grantable or revocable from inside.
  After founding, the payor is ceremonial:
  routine administration never exercises the payor credential,
  keeping the highest-blast-radius credential cold.
- **All citizen administration is governor-wielded and depot-scoped**:
  enfranchise, expel, enfeoff, escheat, recut.
  A governor may enfeoff another citizen of its own depot with the governor capability-set — governors create governors.
  Cross-depot administration does not exist and needs no rule: depot project scoping already forbids it.
  Revisit trigger: an org running many depots wanting delegated cross-depot administration gets a manor-scope steward seated by the payor — federation-era
  (naming caution on that day: *steward* is bound in the cnmp WRC lenses).
- **Governor keeps its quoin.**
  It dissolves structurally: a governor is a citizen holding the governor capability-set;
  mantle ceases to exist as a verb.
  Singleton-governor is a posture, never a code-enforced constraint.
  Standing governor is the default posture for both tiers
  (regression-testable administration, cold payor, tier symmetry);
  an operator may expel theirs between uses
  (known caveat: the on-disk key outlives the escheat by the propagation lag).
- **Identity scope is a tier property.**
  Keyfile citizens are depot-minted: the governor mints the SA in the depot project.
  Federates are IdP-scoped.
  A person in two keyfile depots is two citizens with two keys;
  the one-identity-across-depots story belongs to federation, where the IdP is the census.
  We never build a census.

## The Terrier — a terrier per polity (singular reframe)

Each polity keeps **its own terrier** — one ledger file per depot, payor-created at levy —
hosted together in the **Terrier bucket**, a Manor-homed GCS bucket.
The singular is the metaphor working:
historically every manor kept its own terrier and no realm-wide terrier existed,
so the word itself encodes the no-global-bookkeeping cinch.
Reserve word, not minted: *muniment* (the manorial evidence-of-rights store)
waits for the day the bucket holds non-terrier records; grep-clean when reserved.

- Write ACL: each depot's governors write exactly their own terrier, plus the payor.
  Terrier-write thereby co-locates with enfeoff authority on the same principal — the write-authority invariant satisfied trivially.
- Read population (settled): governors (own-depot) + payor — the admin plane and above.
  Directors, retrievers, and guarded consumers (the Quire memo's choristers) are all outside it;
  widen only when a working verb demonstrates need.
  A citizen holding no permission on the Terrier bucket sees no evidence it exists.
  Never `allUsers`/`allAuthenticatedUsers` — citizen names are reconnaissance data.
- Grain (elected): **managed folders** — per-depot folder IAM delivers own-terrier write and read scoping with one mechanism;
  payor reads all.
- A rights query ("what does X hold in depot Y") is a pure read of one terrier.
- **Terriers are admin-plane only — working verbs never touch them.**
  Directors and retrievers neither read nor write them;
  routine operations go straight to IAM-enforced resources.
- Object versioning on: grant history for free.
- No global bookkeeping: N independent terriers, no cross-depot consistency, no "already registered?" lookup.
- File format: mount-time.

## Verbs and orderings (cinched; words re-minted under MCM Word Selection)

Civic verbs, all governor-wielded.
The 260609 words grant/revoke/rekey were trodden words (grant/revoke are IAM's own vocabulary, maximally grep-hostile);
re-minted into the manorial register that Manor/levy/Terrier already anchor.
Enfeoff/escheat are a genuine tenure pair — creation and lawful reversion of a holding —
so the pair-relationship mirrors the verbs' symmetry.
The endow/enfeoff adjacency is elected consciously:
you *endow* an institution (Quire-side), you *enfeoff* a person (citizen-side);
register separation keeps them audible.

| Verb | Order | Crash leaves |
|---|---|---|
| enfranchise | create SA → register in terrier → mint key **last** | keyless SA (sweepable) or registered keyless citizen (recut completes); never an unregistered key |
| enfeoff | write terrier → apply bindings | visible deficit; re-run enfeoff (idempotent) |
| escheat | withdraw terrier → remove bindings | visible surplus; report-only, safe |
| expel | escheat all held → delete key + SA | partial teardown lands as surplus, never resurrection |
| recut | new key → deliver → verify → delete old | terrier untouched — keys are not its business |
| read-Terrier | pure read | — |

Robustness of the verb set against the two open futures
(the convergence test: change lands only in named, pre-fenced places):

| Verb | Federation arrives | RBRA dropped entirely |
|---|---|---|
| enfranchise / expel | branch by tier (designed for it) | body swaps, name survives |
| enfeoff / escheat | untouched | untouched |
| read-Terrier | untouched | untouched |
| recut | untouched (keyfile-side only) | retires |

RBRA is **demoted, not deleted**: one credential-kind behind the accessor.
Keyfile-tier permanence is itself not promised; the verb set survives either resolution.
Capability-set definitions are code, global across depots; memberships are data, local per depot.
Enfeoff/escheat operate only on named sets, never raw bindings (contamination guard unchanged).

## The audit — MVP is a mirror, not a surgeon

MVP audit: read terriers, read IAM, print the diff.
Report-only — no auto-converge in either direction;
the heal for a deficit is re-running the idempotent enfeoff.
With nothing auto-converging from the terrier, terrier-write is not an escalation path,
which defers the write-authority-vs-grant-authority machinery whole.
The full asymmetric-healing doctrine
(deficit auto-converge, surplus adjudication, member-first axis, expansion gating, etag concurrency — memo-20260605)
remains the cinched future shape, post-MVP.

## Ordering — substrate first (cinched)

1. **Accessor seam.**
   One accessor (likely homed in rba) resolves every token;
   no file outside it names an `RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE` or sources an RBRA file — grep is the gate.
   Identity-keyed interface, role-shaped contents initially:
   call sites keep passing today's role word, reread as a citizen name.
   Honors canon D3 at the interface only:
   contract silent about mint freshness; no cache built (no consumer yet).
   Zero behavior change; suites are the arbiter.
2. **Capability-sets as named code.**
   Transcribe the binding lists out of the invest/mantle bodies; verbs become thin compositions.
   Transcription, not design; zero behavior change.
3. **Civic verbs + terriers.**
   The behavior-changing movement:
   enfranchise/expel/enfeoff/escheat/recut, terriers in the Terrier bucket, identity-only SA names,
   `RBRS_CITIZEN` station selector,
   accessor identity override for multi-identity stations
   (the dev/test rig holds several personas; the assay axis already proves the need).
4. **Handbook rework to the civic ceremony: home open** —
   the stalled handbook heat (₣A6) vs a final movement here; decide once the verbs exist.
   Credential handbook tracks stay frozen meanwhile.

The substrate movements (1, 2) are no-regret under every model variant discussed;
design wobbles cannot reach them.
Their correctness is mechanical (suites + grep), not judgment —
deliberately robust to agent variance across sessions.

## MVP purpose (framing, cinched)

The realistic pioneer is the solo evaluator with their own GCP billing — one human wearing every hat.
The multi-role union citizen
(one person, one identity, one rbra.env, all capability-sets)
**is** the evaluator onboarding experience; that is this heat's MVP case, not an architectural nicety.
Day-after picture:
payor establishes manor, levies depot, seats self-citizen as governor;
that governor enfeoffs the same citizen with director + retriever sets;
`RBRS_CITIZEN` brands the station;
every tabtarget works through the accessor;
read-Terrier prints one line.
invest/divest/mantle/roster no longer exist.

## Inherited concern — governor teardown leak (resolved by dissolution)

With mantle gone and the governor a citizen under the generic verbs,
teardown is expel (escheat-all-first — no tombstone)
and replacement is recut (no delete at all).
The standalone fallback (memo-20260605-governor-mantle-tombstone-leak)
applies only if the civic verbs are descoped.

## Blast radius (cinched)

One citizen = one SA = one key carrying the union of held capability-sets **within its depot** — accepted (memo reasoning stands).
Depot-minted identity bounds the union at the depot:
cross-depot union exposure is federation-tier-shaped (a hijacked live session), never keyfile-shaped (a file at rest).

## What done looks like

- No call site outside the accessor touches credential files or paths (grep-verifiable).
- Capability-sets are named code;
  civic verbs operate on (actor, capability-set, citizen) within a depot;
  terriers function as described;
  a rights query is a read.
- The cult-verb surfaces migrated:
  workstation bash, admin verbs, cloud-side naming, the .adoc specs voicing the old verbs, the theurge cases.
  Handbook migration per the open home decision.
- Suites green at each movement; `complete` before close.

## Cinched decisions

- Enforcement is server-side IAM; the terrier is intent; no file or client-side check is ever the boundary.
- Human-present premise (canon D2) bounds the design;
  no refresh tokens anywhere beyond the payor's own;
  earmarked for RBS0 as an `axk_premise` voicing.
- Payor founds, governors populate, terriers tell, IAM enforces;
  admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into.
- Governor keeps the quoin;
  dissolves to citizen + governor capability-set;
  mantle retires;
  singleton and standing-governor are postures, not constraints.
- Governors create governors within their own depot; cross-depot administration does not exist.
- Identity scope is a tier property:
  keyfile citizens depot-minted; federates IdP-scoped; no census, ever.
- Civic verb words re-minted under MCM Word Selection: enfeoff (was grant), escheat (was revoke), recut (was rekey);
  enfranchise and expel pass the trodden-word gate unchanged;
  endow/enfeoff adjacency elected consciously (institution vs person).
- A terrier per polity, hosted in the Terrier bucket (Manor-homed);
  payor creates at levy;
  governor-writeable own-terrier-only;
  read population governors-and-above, exclusion list explicit (directors, retrievers, guarded consumers);
  managed-folder grain;
  working verbs never touch terriers;
  *muniment* held in reserve, unminted.
- Capability-set definitions global (code); memberships local (data);
  enfeoff/escheat only on named sets, never raw bindings.
- Intent-first orderings as tabled; key-last enfranchise.
- MVP audit is a read-only diff; the asymmetric-healing doctrine is deferred intact, not diluted.
- RBRA is demoted to one credential-kind behind the accessor — not deleted; permanence not promised either.
- Multi-role is first-class; the solo evaluator's union citizen is the MVP case.
- No migration machinery: pre-MVP has no compatibility surface.
- The accessor is identity-keyed at the interface from day one; D3's no-per-call-mint assumption honored at the interface only.

## Retired cinches

- "Roster derives from IAM, not a stored list" — retired earlier (see memo); the Terrier + audit replaced it.
  The true invariant (no enforcement state outside IAM) survives.
- "Manor hosts the Terrier" as a singular registry — retired for per-depot terriers
  (still physically Manor-hosted as a bucket, but no global census;
  both the singular-registry framing and a briefly-explored manor-scoped-citizen-SA model are dropped).
- "Keyfile/solo drops or demotes the standing governor" — retired:
  standing governor is the default posture;
  the true invariant (minimize standing privilege) survives as the posture knob.
- "One SA per person" globally — narrowed: one SA per person **per depot**;
  the cross-depot one-identity property is federation's, via the IdP.
- The memo-20260527 stepping-stone question (role-keyed vs identity-keyed accessor) — resolved:
  identity-keyed interface, role-shaped contents.
- The 260609 verb words grant/revoke/rekey — re-minted (see Verbs section); the verb *semantics* carried over unchanged.

## Open — resolve within the heat

- Handbook rework home: ₣A6 vs a final movement here.
- Terrier file format (reader population, grain, and bucket naming are settled above).
- Whether enfranchise+enfeoff get a convenience wrapper for the one-hat citizen.
- Whether recut ships in MVP or immediately after.
- RBS0 incorporation timing for the civic quoins (citizen, terrier, capability-set, the human-present premise);
  at incorporation, decide whether the citizen's terrier entry gets a noun
  (the prebend's manorial dual — candidate *feoffment*)
  and the read-Terrier verb word (candidate *consult*).
- Breadcrumbs owed:
  the bedrock Quire memo's verb lines predate the re-mint
  (§12 "enfranchised, granted, revoked, expelled" and "Establishment (invest/divest/roster/endow)") — owe it the civic-verb breadcrumb;
  the canon's diagram repoint item stands.
- `RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC` eviction to RBRD lifetime policy (canon D4/D5) — small, slot it where convenient.

## Sources

rbk-08-credential-repairs;
memo-20260604-credential-churn-leak-and-propagation-races (the lifecycle split — the enabler);
memo-20260527-operator-credential-models (folded into the federation canon);
memo-20260605-governor-mantle-tombstone-leak (standalone fallback only);
memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model (mechanism, partially superseded as noted);
memo-20260605-citizen-model-ultracode-process;
memo-20260609-federation-canon (D1–D6; the tier boundary and the premise);
MCM "Word Selection" + `mcm_asterism`/`mcm_trodden_word` (the verb re-mint doctrine);
RBSHR "Operator federation".
Prior revisions: 260605 design conversation + two review passes; 260606 Manor/verb dispositions; 260609 morning Terrier naming; 260609 evening civic structure, per-depot Terrier, substrate ordering.
This 260610 revision (groom session following the MCM Word Selection landing):
civic verb re-mint (enfeoff/escheat/recut),
terrier singular reframe + Terrier bucket + managed-folder grain,
read-population settlement (governors and above, explicit exclusions),
muniment reserve, endow/enfeoff conscious adjacency, feoffment/consult queued for RBS0 incorporation,
bedrock-memo breadcrumb queued.
Operator commitment: this paddock and its paces are slated by Fable-class agents; density is calibrated accordingly.

4. **Handbook rework to the civic ceremony: home open** —
   the stalled handbook heat (₣A6) vs a final movement here; decide once the verbs exist.
   Credential handbook tracks stay frozen meanwhile.

The substrate movements (1, 2) are no-regret under every model variant discussed;
design wobbles cannot reach them.
Their correctness is mechanical (suites + grep), not judgment —
deliberately robust to agent variance across sessions.

## MVP purpose (framing, cinched)

The realistic pioneer is the solo evaluator with their own GCP billing — one human wearing every hat.
The multi-role union citizen
(one person, one identity, one rbra.env, all capability-sets)
**is** the evaluator onboarding experience; that is this heat's MVP case, not an architectural nicety.
Day-after picture:
payor establishes manor, levies depot, seats self-citizen as governor;
that governor enfeoffs the same citizen with director + retriever sets;
`RBRS_CITIZEN` brands the station;
every tabtarget works through the accessor;
read-Terrier prints one line.
invest/divest/mantle/roster no longer exist.

## Inherited concern — governor teardown leak (resolved by dissolution)

With mantle gone and the governor a citizen under the generic verbs,
teardown is expel (escheat-all-first — no tombstone)
and replacement is recut (no delete at all).
The standalone fallback (memo-20260605-governor-mantle-tombstone-leak)
applies only if the civic verbs are descoped.

## Blast radius (cinched)

One citizen = one SA = one key carrying the union of held capability-sets **within its depot** — accepted (memo reasoning stands).
Depot-minted identity bounds the union at the depot:
cross-depot union exposure is federation-tier-shaped (a hijacked live session), never keyfile-shaped (a file at rest).

## What done looks like

- No call site outside the accessor touches credential files or paths (grep-verifiable).
- Capability-sets are named code;
  civic verbs operate on (actor, capability-set, citizen) within a depot;
  terriers function as described;
  a rights query is a read.
- The cult-verb surfaces migrated:
  workstation bash, admin verbs, cloud-side naming, the .adoc specs voicing the old verbs, the theurge cases.
  Handbook migration per the open home decision.
- Suites green at each movement; `complete` before close.

## Cinched decisions

- Enforcement is server-side IAM; the terrier is intent; no file or client-side check is ever the boundary.
- Human-present premise (canon D2) bounds the design;
  no refresh tokens anywhere beyond the payor's own;
  earmarked for RBS0 as an `axk_premise` voicing.
- Payor founds, governors populate, terriers tell, IAM enforces;
  admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into.
- Governor keeps the quoin;
  dissolves to citizen + governor capability-set;
  mantle retires;
  singleton and standing-governor are postures, not constraints.
- Governors create governors within their own depot; cross-depot administration does not exist.
- Identity scope is a tier property:
  keyfile citizens depot-minted; federates IdP-scoped; no census, ever.
- Civic verb words re-minted under MCM Word Selection: enfeoff (was grant), escheat (was revoke), recut (was rekey);
  enfranchise and expel pass the trodden-word gate unchanged;
  endow/enfeoff adjacency elected consciously (institution vs person).
- A terrier per polity, hosted in the Terrier bucket (Manor-homed);
  payor creates at levy;
  governor-writeable own-terrier-only;
  read population governors-and-above, exclusion list explicit (directors, retrievers, guarded consumers);
  managed-folder grain;
  working verbs never touch terriers;
  *muniment* held in reserve, unminted.
- Capability-set definitions global (code); memberships local (data);
  enfeoff/escheat only on named sets, never raw bindings.
- Intent-first orderings as tabled; key-last enfranchise.
- MVP audit is a read-only diff; the asymmetric-healing doctrine is deferred intact, not diluted.
- RBRA is demoted to one credential-kind behind the accessor — not deleted; permanence not promised either.
- Multi-role is first-class; the solo evaluator's union citizen is the MVP case.
- No migration machinery: pre-MVP has no compatibility surface.
- The accessor is identity-keyed at the interface from day one; D3's no-per-call-mint assumption honored at the interface only.

## Retired cinches

- "Roster derives from IAM, not a stored list" — retired earlier (see memo); the Terrier + audit replaced it.
  The true invariant (no enforcement state outside IAM) survives.
- "Manor hosts the Terrier" as a singular registry — retired for per-depot terriers
  (still physically Manor-hosted as a bucket, but no global census;
  both the singular-registry framing and a briefly-explored manor-scoped-citizen-SA model are dropped).
- "Keyfile/solo drops or demotes the standing governor" — retired:
  standing governor is the default posture;
  the true invariant (minimize standing privilege) survives as the posture knob.
- "One SA per person" globally — narrowed: one SA per person **per depot**;
  the cross-depot one-identity property is federation's, via the IdP.
- The memo-20260527 stepping-stone question (role-keyed vs identity-keyed accessor) — resolved:
  identity-keyed interface, role-shaped contents.
- The 260609 verb words grant/revoke/rekey — re-minted (see Verbs section); the verb *semantics* carried over unchanged.

## Open — resolve within the heat

- Handbook rework home: ₣A6 vs a final movement here.
- Terrier file format (reader population, grain, and bucket naming are settled above).
- Whether enfranchise+enfeoff get a convenience wrapper for the one-hat citizen.
- Whether recut ships in MVP or immediately after.
- RBS0 incorporation timing for the civic quoins (citizen, terrier, capability-set, the human-present premise);
  at incorporation, decide whether the citizen's terrier entry gets a noun
  (the prebend's manorial dual — candidate *feoffment*)
  and the read-Terrier verb word (candidate *consult*).
- Breadcrumbs owed:
  the bedrock Quire memo's verb lines predate the re-mint
  (§12 "enfranchised, granted, revoked, expelled" and "Establishment (invest/divest/roster/endow)") — owe it the civic-verb breadcrumb;
  the canon's diagram repoint item stands.
- `RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC` eviction to RBRD lifetime policy (canon D4/D5) — small, slot it where convenient.

## Sources

rbk-08-credential-repairs;
memo-20260604-credential-churn-leak-and-propagation-races (the lifecycle split — the enabler);
memo-20260527-operator-credential-models (folded into the federation canon);
memo-20260605-governor-mantle-tombstone-leak (standalone fallback only);
memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model (mechanism, partially superseded as noted);
memo-20260605-citizen-model-ultracode-process;
memo-20260609-federation-canon (D1–D6; the tier boundary and the premise);
MCM "Word Selection" + `mcm_asterism`/`mcm_trodden_word` (the verb re-mint doctrine);
RBSHR "Operator federation".
Prior revisions: 260605 design conversation + two review passes; 260606 Manor/verb dispositions; 260609 morning Terrier naming; 260609 evening civic structure, per-depot Terrier, substrate ordering.
This 260610 revision (groom session following the MCM Word Selection landing):
civic verb re-mint (enfeoff/escheat/recut),
terrier singular reframe + Terrier bucket + managed-folder grain,
read-population settlement (governors and above, explicit exclusions),
muniment reserve, endow/enfeoff conscious adjacency, feoffment/consult queued for RBS0 incorporation,
bedrock-memo breadcrumb queued.
Operator commitment: this paddock and its paces are slated by Fable-class agents; density is calibrated accordingly.

content.text:
Breadcrumb discharged and notched (`68999e069`). Now the colophon question — here's the expansion and my opinion.

**What I meant at groom:** today's service-account administration lives in the `rbw-a` "Accounts" family — `rbw-aM` PayorMantlesGovernor, `rbw-arI`/`rbw-adI` invests, `rbw-arD`/`rbw-adD` divests, `rbw-arr`/`rbw-adr` rosters. Every one of those colophons dies with the cult verbs — and notice the family is *doubly* role-era: the family letter says "accounts" (the SA is the framing) and the second letter is the role key (`r` retriever, `d` director), which the citizen model abolishes. There is nothing to migrate; the family needs replacement, not renovation.

**Your musing is exactly right, and it yields the design rule.** The family letter should name the *tier-blind domain noun*, so that when federation arrives, verb *bodies* branch and colophons never move. The existing families already group by domain noun (`c` crucible, `d` depot, `f` foundry, `l` lode, `r` regime, `t` theurge) — never by actor, never by mechanism. The mechanism-flavored noun ("accounts") is precisely what rots: federates have no service accounts at all. The noun that survives both tiers and both clouds is the one your own paddock elevated: **polity**. Admission, tenure, reversion, expulsion, the terrier — all polity administration, whether the polity is a depot (GCP IAM) or someday a Cloister (AWS IAM), keyfile or federated.

**Opinion: mint `rbw-p` — the polity family.** The letter is free, the grouping passes the convergence test (federation lands inside verb bodies, the launcher surface is untouched), and it gives the founding gestures a home too — "seat the first governor" is a payor-wielded polity verb, not an account operation. Sketch of the retirement map:

| Today (`rbw-a`, retiring) | Successor (`rbw-p`) |
|---|---|
| `rbw-arI`/`rbw-adI` invest | enfranchise + enfeoff (two verbs now — admission and tenure decomposed) |
| `rbw-arD`/`rbw-adD` divest | escheat / expel |
| `rbw-arr`/`rbw-adr` roster | terrier read (*consult*, if elected) |
| `rbw-aM` mantle | retires whole; founding seat rides levy-time polity verbs |
| — | recut, audit (new surface) |

Two wrinkles, named now so the mount-time mint doesn't trip:

- **The payor-misread risk.** `p` could be skimmed as "payor" — payor procedures already wear capital-P imprints (`rbw-gPI`, `rbw-gPE`, `rbw-gPR`). I think the risk is acceptable: frontispieces disambiguate instantly, and "polity" is the honest initial where every alternative ("civic" → `c` taken by crucible, "citizen" likewise, "terrier" → `t` taken by theurge) is blocked. But it deserved saying out loud.
- **The all-'e' pileup.** The manorial register loves its `e`: enfranchise, enfeoff, escheat, expel. The within-family second letters can't be initials and must be minted for early divergence (something like `pn`/`pf`/`ps`/`px` — settled at the zipper with its sub-letter legend, mount-time).

One deliberate exclusion: the `rbw-ac` access probes survive the cult-verb purge — they're accessor-seam diagnostics, not polity administration — so they stay put, get reworded to citizen identity during movement 1, and their long-term family is a question for the accessor seam, not for `rbw-p`.

If `rbw-p` sits right, I'd record the election and the retirement map in the paddock (it's exactly the kind of pre-fenced naming movement 3 wants already settled), and then the heat is ready for slating movements 1–2.

message.content: rbw-p sits right for the polity, but it does make me want to replace the Payor ones with rbw-P; thoughts?  Does tab completion on all platforms obey case sensitivity?  Not crtiti8cal if it doesn't but it might affect my choice here.

content.text:
Direct answer first, since it gates the choice: **no — tab completion does not uniformly obey case across your fleet.**

- **bash and zsh defaults are case-sensitive** (your macOS zsh included), so `rbw-p<TAB>` and `rbw-P<TAB>` are distinct there.
- **Git for Windows ships `completion-ignore-case on`** in its default inputrc, and user dotfiles flip it on frequently everywhere else — on those shells, `rbw-p<TAB>` offers *both* families.
- One layer deeper: on macOS (default APFS) and Windows, the **filesystem itself resolves wrong-case paths**, so `tt/rbw-p.Foo.sh` would happily *execute* a file named `rbw-P.Foo.sh`. Only Linux (cerebro) is strict end to end.

So case carries identity reliably on exactly one of your three platforms. The repo already lives with this — `rbw-i` Image vs `rbw-I` Ifrit — and it works there because the two families are semantically *unrelated*: when the completion list interleaves them, nothing plausible mis-binds.

That's precisely why I'd advise **against `rbw-P`**: payor and polity are semantically *adjacent* (the payor founds polities, seats governors, creates terriers), and a case-only discriminator between adjacent meanings is the weakest pairing on the least reliable channel — the colophon-scale version of the `axhempt_`/`axhopt_` interior-letter failure the introspection memo flags. On a Git Bash box, `rbw-p<TAB>` would interleave founding ceremonies with citizen administration, and the reader's "which family am I in" signal is one character of case that two of three platforms don't honor.

Two further thoughts:

- **There's no forcing event for the payor colophons.** The civic model retires `rbw-a` wholesale, but `rbw-gPI`/`rbw-gPE`/`rbw-gPR` and `rbw-dL`/`rbw-dU`/`rbw-dl` survive this heat with their bodies intact (levy gains the create-terrier and seat-governor gestures, but that's body work, not a rename). Their current homes are honest: the `gP*` trio are guided ceremonies — Guide is an interaction-style family — and the `d*` trio are depot lifecycle. Regrouping them now is churn the heat doesn't need.
- **If you do want the payor surface gathered someday, the better mint is `rbw-m` — Manor.** It names the domain rather than the actor (consistent with every other family), it's the payor's actual seat — establish manor, levy depots *from* the manor, the manor's OAuth credential, the manor-homed Terrier bucket — and it diverges from `p` at the first character on every platform. It does case-pair with `rbw-M` Marshal, but that's the unrelated-pair shape `i`/`I` already proves tolerable.

So my recommendation stands at: mint `rbw-p` for the polity family now, leave payor colophons untouched this heat, and hold `rbw-m` Manor as the named reserve if a payor/founding regroup ever earns its forcing event. Want me to fold the `rbw-p` election, the retirement map, and the `rbw-m` reserve into the paddock?

message.content: yes, and indeed lets plan rbw-m transformation in this heat, with retirement map including that.  Update paddock now if no more questions.  Then: can you dispatch a Fable agent to read the paddock and ask questions?  That seems an excellent way to determine if we have really made the paddock standalone.

input.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣BZ

## Boundary — shape, not mechanism (cinched)

This heat builds the **citizen tier** — the keyfile, no-org operator-credential tier —
restructured so its capability layer is identical to the future federation tier's.
The backing stays keyfile; a no-org operator runs it unchanged.
We do **not** build federation here.
OUT of scope: workforce pool/provider setup, STS exchange, IdP device flow,
RBRD mode-enum activation,
the full drift-audit machinery,
and `modifiedGrantsByRole` topology conditioning —
all post-MVP (RBSHR "Operator federation"; `Memos/memo-20260609-federation-canon.md`; audit section below).

Pre-MVP there is **no compatibility surface**: no depots exist that we must migrate.
The grandfather bootstrap (role-from-SA-name recovery) is retired unbuilt.

Mechanism lives in `Memos/memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model.md`
(superseded-in-part banner landed; consult the paddock first on Terrier homing, census, mantle, migration — and on the verb words, re-minted below)
and `Memos/memo-20260609-federation-canon.md`; this paddock holds shape.

## The premise — human present (cinched; earmarked for RBS0)

Canon D2, restated as the bounding premise:
**a human is present at the kickoff of every run; no run outlives a session cap.**
Therefore the system holds no refresh token anywhere beyond the payor's own,
ships no unattended-run support,
and an orchestrating agent never holds a secret
(device-flow kickoff surfaces only user_code + verification URI).
This belongs in RBS0 as a premise quoin voiced `axk_premise` when the civic vocabulary incorporates —
it is the constraint that bounds the entire credential design.
The unchosen middle credential path — a bash-managed per-operator OAuth refresh token —
is recorded as declined under this premise
(a durable secret at rest, plus the OAuth-client ceremony multiplied across operators);
no current revisit trigger.

## The civic structure (cinched)

**Payor founds, governors populate, terriers tell, IAM enforces.**

- **Admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into.**
  The payor's founding gestures: establish manor, levy depot, create the depot's terrier, seat the first governor.
  The payor is outside the citizenry — no terrier entry; its authority is constitutive, not grantable or revocable from inside.
  After founding, the payor is ceremonial:
  routine administration never exercises the payor credential,
  keeping the highest-blast-radius credential cold.
- **All citizen administration is governor-wielded and depot-scoped**:
  enfranchise, expel, enfeoff, escheat, recut.
  A governor may enfeoff another citizen of its own depot with the governor capability-set — governors create governors.
  Cross-depot administration does not exist and needs no rule: depot project scoping already forbids it.
  Revisit trigger: an org running many depots wanting delegated cross-depot administration gets a manor-scope steward seated by the payor — federation-era
  (naming caution on that day: *steward* is bound in the cnmp WRC lenses).
- **Governor keeps its quoin.**
  It dissolves structurally: a governor is a citizen holding the governor capability-set;
  mantle ceases to exist as a verb.
  Singleton-governor is a posture, never a code-enforced constraint.
  Standing governor is the default posture for both tiers
  (regression-testable administration, cold payor, tier symmetry);
  an operator may expel theirs between uses
  (known caveat: the on-disk key outlives the escheat by the propagation lag).
- **Identity scope is a tier property.**
  Keyfile citizens are depot-minted: the governor mints the SA in the depot project.
  Federates are IdP-scoped.
  A person in two keyfile depots is two citizens with two keys;
  the one-identity-across-depots story belongs to federation, where the IdP is the census.
  We never build a census.

## The Terrier — a terrier per polity (singular reframe)

Each polity keeps **its own terrier** — one ledger file per depot, payor-created at levy —
hosted together in the **Terrier bucket**, a Manor-homed GCS bucket.
The singular is the metaphor working:
historically every manor kept its own terrier and no realm-wide terrier existed,
so the word itself encodes the no-global-bookkeeping cinch.
Reserve word, not minted: *muniment* (the manorial evidence-of-rights store)
waits for the day the bucket holds non-terrier records; grep-clean when reserved.

- Write ACL: each depot's governors write exactly their own terrier, plus the payor.
  Terrier-write thereby co-locates with enfeoff authority on the same principal — the write-authority invariant satisfied trivially.
- Read population (settled): governors (own-depot) + payor — the admin plane and above.
  Directors, retrievers, and guarded consumers (the Quire memo's choristers) are all outside it;
  widen only when a working verb demonstrates need.
  A citizen holding no permission on the Terrier bucket sees no evidence it exists.
  Never `allUsers`/`allAuthenticatedUsers` — citizen names are reconnaissance data.
- Grain (elected): **managed folders** — per-depot folder IAM delivers own-terrier write and read scoping with one mechanism;
  payor reads all.
- A rights query ("what does X hold in depot Y") is a pure read of one terrier.
- **Terriers are admin-plane only — working verbs never touch them.**
  Directors and retrievers neither read nor write them;
  routine operations go straight to IAM-enforced resources.
- Object versioning on: grant history for free.
- No global bookkeeping: N independent terriers, no cross-depot consistency, no "already registered?" lookup.
- File format: mount-time.

## Verbs and orderings (cinched; words re-minted under MCM Word Selection)

Civic verbs, all governor-wielded.
The 260609 words grant/revoke/rekey were trodden words (grant/revoke are IAM's own vocabulary, maximally grep-hostile);
re-minted into the manorial register that Manor/levy/Terrier already anchor.
Enfeoff/escheat are a genuine tenure pair — creation and lawful reversion of a holding —
so the pair-relationship mirrors the verbs' symmetry.
The endow/enfeoff adjacency is elected consciously:
you *endow* an institution (Quire-side), you *enfeoff* a person (citizen-side);
register separation keeps them audible.

| Verb | Order | Crash leaves |
|---|---|---|
| enfranchise | create SA → register in terrier → mint key **last** | keyless SA (sweepable) or registered keyless citizen (recut completes); never an unregistered key |
| enfeoff | write terrier → apply bindings | visible deficit; re-run enfeoff (idempotent) |
| escheat | withdraw terrier → remove bindings | visible surplus; report-only, safe |
| expel | escheat all held → delete key + SA | partial teardown lands as surplus, never resurrection |
| recut | new key → deliver → verify → delete old | terrier untouched — keys are not its business |
| read-Terrier | pure read | — |

Robustness of the verb set against the two open futures
(the convergence test: change lands only in named, pre-fenced places):

| Verb | Federation arrives | RBRA dropped entirely |
|---|---|---|
| enfranchise / expel | branch by tier (designed for it) | body swaps, name survives |
| enfeoff / escheat | untouched | untouched |
| read-Terrier | untouched | untouched |
| recut | untouched (keyfile-side only) | retires |

RBRA is **demoted, not deleted**: one credential-kind behind the accessor.
Keyfile-tier permanence is itself not promised; the verb set survives either resolution.
Capability-set definitions are code, global across depots; memberships are data, local per depot.
Enfeoff/escheat operate only on named sets, never raw bindings (contamination guard unchanged).

## Launcher surface — colophon families (elected)

Two family mints, one rejection:

- **`rbw-p` — the polity family** (minted): governor-wielded citizen administration —
  enfranchise, enfeoff, escheat, expel, recut, terrier read, audit.
  The family letter names the tier-blind domain noun,
  so federation changes verb bodies, never colophons — the convergence test applied to the launcher surface.
- **`rbw-m` — the manor family** (minted; regroup executes this heat): the payor's founding surface,
  gathered from its current scatter across `rbw-g` and `rbw-d` (see map).
  Levy gains the create-terrier and seat-first-governor founding gestures here.
  Director-wielded depot verbs (`rbw-di`, `rbw-dI`, `rbw-dY`) stay in `rbw-d`.
- **`rbw-P` rejected**: payor and polity are semantically adjacent,
  and a case-only family discriminator is unreliable across the fleet
  (Git Bash ships completion-ignore-case; macOS and Windows filesystems resolve wrong-case paths).
  Case-pair families are reserved for semantically unrelated pairs —
  the `rbw-i`/`rbw-I` precedent; `rbw-m` vs `rbw-M` (Marshal) rides the same allowance.
- **`rbw-a` retires whole** with the cult verbs.
  The `rbw-ac` access probes survive in place
  (accessor-seam diagnostics, not polity administration), reworded to citizen identity in movement 1;
  their long-term family belongs to the accessor seam.

Retirement map:

| Today | Fate |
|---|---|
| `rbw-arI` / `rbw-adI` (invest) | `rbw-p` enfranchise + enfeoff |
| `rbw-arD` / `rbw-adD` (divest) | `rbw-p` escheat / expel |
| `rbw-arr` / `rbw-adr` (roster) | `rbw-p` terrier read |
| `rbw-aM` (mantle) | retires whole; founding seat rides `rbw-m` levy |
| `rbw-gPI` / `rbw-gPE` / `rbw-gPR` (payor OAuth ceremonies) | `rbw-m` (move; verbs unchanged) |
| `rbw-dL` / `rbw-dU` / `rbw-dl` (depot levy/unmake/list) | `rbw-m` (move; levy gains terrier + seat gestures) |
| — | `rbw-p` recut, audit (new surface) |

Sub-letter caution for the `rbw-p` mint: the manorial verbs pile on 'e'
(enfranchise, enfeoff, escheat, expel) —
second letters must diverge early, minted at the zipper with its legend.

## The audit — MVP is a mirror, not a surgeon

MVP audit: read terriers, read IAM, print the diff.
Report-only — no auto-converge in either direction;
the heal for a deficit is re-running the idempotent enfeoff.
With nothing auto-converging from the terrier, terrier-write is not an escalation path,
which defers the write-authority-vs-grant-authority machinery whole.
The full asymmetric-healing doctrine
(deficit auto-converge, surplus adjudication, member-first axis, expansion gating, etag concurrency — memo-20260605)
remains the cinched future shape, post-MVP.

## Ordering — substrate first (cinched)

1. **Accessor seam.**
   One accessor (likely homed in rba) resolves every token;
   no file outside it names an `RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE` or sources an RBRA file — grep is the gate.
   Identity-keyed interface, role-shaped contents initially:
   call sites keep passing today's role word, reread as a citizen name.
   Honors canon D3 at the interface only:
   contract silent about mint freshness; no cache built (no consumer yet).
   Zero behavior change; suites are the arbiter.
2. **Capability-sets as named code.**
   Transcribe the binding lists out of the invest/mantle bodies; verbs become thin compositions.
   Transcription, not design; zero behavior change.
3. **Civic verbs + terriers.**
   The behavior-changing movement:
   enfranchise/expel/enfeoff/escheat/recut under the `rbw-p` family, terriers in the Terrier bucket, identity-only SA names,
   `RBRS_CITIZEN` station selector,
   accessor identity override for multi-identity stations
   (the dev/test rig holds several personas; the assay axis already proves the need).
4. **Manor colophon regroup.**
   The payor surface gathers into `rbw-m` per the retirement map;
   mechanical (zipper + tabtarget renames + reference sweep);
   sequenced ahead of the handbook movement so the credential tracks unfreeze against final names.
5. **Handbook rework to the civic ceremony: home open** —
   the stalled handbook heat (₣A6) vs a final movement here; decide once the verbs exist.
   Credential handbook tracks stay frozen meanwhile.

The substrate movements (1, 2) are no-regret under every model variant discussed;
design wobbles cannot reach them.
Their correctness is mechanical (suites + grep), not judgment —
deliberately robust to agent variance across sessions.

## MVP purpose (framing, cinched)

The realistic pioneer is the solo evaluator with their own GCP billing — one human wearing every hat.
The multi-role union citizen
(one person, one identity, one rbra.env, all capability-sets)
**is** the evaluator onboarding experience; that is this heat's MVP case, not an architectural nicety.
Day-after picture:
payor establishes manor, levies depot, seats self-citizen as governor;
that governor enfeoffs the same citizen with director + retriever sets;
`RBRS_CITIZEN` brands the station;
every tabtarget works through the accessor;
read-Terrier prints one line.
invest/divest/mantle/roster no longer exist.

## Inherited concern — governor teardown leak (resolved by dissolution)

With mantle gone and the governor a citizen under the generic verbs,
teardown is expel (escheat-all-first — no tombstone)
and replacement is recut (no delete at all).
The standalone fallback (memo-20260605-governor-mantle-tombstone-leak)
applies only if the civic verbs are descoped.

## Blast radius (cinched)

One citizen = one SA = one key carrying the union of held capability-sets **within its depot** — accepted (memo reasoning stands).
Depot-minted identity bounds the union at the depot:
cross-depot union exposure is federation-tier-shaped (a hijacked live session), never keyfile-shaped (a file at rest).

## What done looks like

- No call site outside the accessor touches credential files or paths (grep-verifiable).
- Capability-sets are named code;
  civic verbs operate on (actor, capability-set, citizen) within a depot;
  terriers function as described;
  a rights query is a read.
- The launcher surface reflects the elected families:
  `rbw-a` gone, `rbw-p` live, the payor verbs under `rbw-m`.
- The cult-verb surfaces migrated:
  workstation bash, admin verbs, cloud-side naming, the .adoc specs voicing the old verbs, the theurge cases.
  Handbook migration per the open home decision.
- Suites green at each movement; `complete` before close.

## Cinched decisions

- Enforcement is server-side IAM; the terrier is intent; no file or client-side check is ever the boundary.
- Human-present premise (canon D2) bounds the design;
  no refresh tokens anywhere beyond the payor's own;
  earmarked for RBS0 as an `axk_premise` voicing.
- Payor founds, governors populate, terriers tell, IAM enforces;
  admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into.
- Governor keeps the quoin;
  dissolves to citizen + governor capability-set;
  mantle retires;
  singleton and standing-governor are postures, not constraints.
- Governors create governors within their own depot; cross-depot administration does not exist.
- Identity scope is a tier property:
  keyfile citizens depot-minted; federates IdP-scoped; no census, ever.
- Civic verb words re-minted under MCM Word Selection: enfeoff (was grant), escheat (was revoke), recut (was rekey);
  enfranchise and expel pass the trodden-word gate unchanged;
  endow/enfeoff adjacency elected consciously (institution vs person).
- A terrier per polity, hosted in the Terrier bucket (Manor-homed);
  payor creates at levy;
  governor-writeable own-terrier-only;
  read population governors-and-above, exclusion list explicit (directors, retrievers, guarded consumers);
  managed-folder grain;
  working verbs never touch terriers;
  *muniment* held in reserve, unminted.
- Colophon families: `rbw-p` polity (tier-blind — federation never moves colophons);
  `rbw-m` manor regroup executes this heat;
  `rbw-P` rejected on case-adjacency;
  case-pair families only for semantically unrelated meanings.
- Capability-set definitions global (code); memberships local (data);
  enfeoff/escheat only on named sets, never raw bindings.
- Intent-first orderings as tabled; key-last enfranchise.
- MVP audit is a read-only diff; the asymmetric-healing doctrine is deferred intact, not diluted.
- RBRA is demoted to one credential-kind behind the accessor — not deleted; permanence not promised either.
- Multi-role is first-class; the solo evaluator's union citizen is the MVP case.
- No migration machinery: pre-MVP has no compatibility surface.
- The accessor is identity-keyed at the interface from day one; D3's no-per-call-mint assumption honored at the interface only.

## Retired cinches

- "Roster derives from IAM, not a stored list" — retired earlier (see memo); the Terrier + audit replaced it.
  The true invariant (no enforcement state outside IAM) survives.
- "Manor hosts the Terrier" as a singular registry — retired for per-depot terriers
  (still physically Manor-hosted as a bucket, but no global census;
  both the singular-registry framing and a briefly-explored manor-scoped-citizen-SA model are dropped).
- "Keyfile/solo drops or demotes the standing governor" — retired:
  standing governor is the default posture;
  the true invariant (minimize standing privilege) survives as the posture knob.
- "One SA per person" globally — narrowed: one SA per person **per depot**;
  the cross-depot one-identity property is federation's, via the IdP.
- The memo-20260527 stepping-stone question (role-keyed vs identity-keyed accessor) — resolved:
  identity-keyed interface, role-shaped contents.
- The 260609 verb words grant/revoke/rekey — re-minted (see Verbs section); the verb *semantics* carried over unchanged.

## Open — resolve within the heat

- Handbook rework home: ₣A6 vs a final movement here.
- Terrier file format (reader population, grain, and bucket naming are settled above).
- Whether enfranchise+enfeoff get a convenience wrapper for the one-hat citizen.
- Whether recut ships in MVP or immediately after.
- `rbw-gq` (quota capacity review) disposition: guide vs manor — decide at the regroup.
- RBS0 incorporation timing for the civic quoins (citizen, terrier, capability-set, the human-present premise);
  at incorporation, decide whether the citizen's terrier entry gets a noun
  (the prebend's manorial dual — candidate *feoffment*)
  and the read-Terrier verb word (candidate *consult*).
- Breadcrumbs owed: the canon's diagram repoint item stands
  (the bedrock memo's verb-line breadcrumb is discharged).
- `RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC` eviction to RBRD lifetime policy (canon D4/D5) — small, slot it where convenient.

## Sources

rbk-08-credential-repairs;
memo-20260604-credential-churn-leak-and-propagation-races (the lifecycle split — the enabler);
memo-20260527-operator-credential-models (folded into the federation canon);
memo-20260605-governor-mantle-tombstone-leak (standalone fallback only);
memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model (mechanism, partially superseded as noted);
memo-20260605-citizen-model-ultracode-process;
memo-20260609-federation-canon (D1–D6; the tier boundary and the premise);
MCM "Word Selection" + `mcm_asterism`/`mcm_trodden_word` (the verb re-mint doctrine);
RBSHR "Operator federation".
Prior revisions: 260605 design conversation + two review passes; 260606 Manor/verb dispositions; 260609 morning Terrier naming; 260609 evening civic structure, per-depot Terrier, substrate ordering.
This 260610 revision (groom session following the MCM Word Selection landing):
civic verb re-mint (enfeoff/escheat/recut),
terrier singular reframe + Terrier bucket + managed-folder grain,
read-population settlement (governors and above, explicit exclusions),
muniment reserve, endow/enfeoff conscious adjacency, feoffment/consult queued for RBS0 incorporation,
colophon family election (`rbw-p` polity, `rbw-m` manor regroup in-heat, `rbw-P` rejected),
bedrock-memo breadcrumb discharged.
Operator commitment: this paddock and its paces are slated by Fable-class agents; density is calibrated accordingly.

| `rbw-dL` / `rbw-dU` / `rbw-dl` (depot levy/unmake/list) | `rbw-m` (move; levy gains terrier + seat gestures) |
| — | `rbw-p` recut, audit (new surface) |

Sub-letter caution for the `rbw-p` mint: the manorial verbs pile on 'e'
(enfranchise, enfeoff, escheat, expel) —
second letters must diverge early, minted at the zipper with its legend.

## The audit — MVP is a mirror, not a surgeon

MVP audit: read terriers, read IAM, print the diff.
Report-only — no auto-converge in either direction;
the heal for a deficit is re-running the idempotent enfeoff.

which defers the write-authority-vs-grant-authority machinery whole.
The full asymmetric-healing doctrine
(deficit auto-converge, surplus adjudication, member-first axis, expansion gating, etag concurrency — memo-20260605)
remains the cinched future shape, post-MVP.

## Ordering — substrate first (cinched)

1. **Accessor seam.**
   One accessor (likely homed in rba) resolves every token;
   no file outside it names an `RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE` or sources an RBRA file — grep is the gate.
